mission.title: cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s-: Support compatibility with AWS CDK v2 / upgrade to constructs v10
mission.description: Support compatibility with AWS CDK v2 / upgrade to constructs v10. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- upgrade symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s-
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- -n cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s-
kubectl get configmap -n cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s-
```
[More info](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cdk/latest/guide/work-with-cdk-v2.html)

I was considering migrating to AWS CDK v2, however it looks like when attempting to install cdk8s in the same virtualenv that it can't solve an appropriate version of

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support compatibility with AWS CDK v2 / upgrade to…
steps.description:
This should now be resolved. Latest version of cdk8s+ only support constructs 10, and for the cdk8s core library, use cdk8s v1 for working with constructs v3 (e.g v1 of AWS CDK), and use cdk8s v2 for working with constructs v10 (e.g AWS CDK v2)
```yaml
# requirements.txt

aws-cdk-lib>=2.0.0rc1
constructs>=10.0.0
cdk8s
```

steps.title: Upgrade cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s-:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s-/cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- --namespace cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s-
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s-
helm list -n cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s-
```

steps.title: Confirm Support compatibility with AWS CDK v2 / upgrade… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- -n cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: This should now be resolved. Latest version of cdk8s+ only support constructs 10, and for the cdk8s core library, use cdk8s v1 for working with constructs v3 (e.g v1 of AWS CDK), and use cdk8s v2 for working with constructs v10 (e.g AWS CDK v2)
codeSnippets:
ERROR: Cannot install -r requirements.txt (line 1), -r requirements.txt (line 3), -r requirements.txt (line 4), cdk8s, cdk8s-plus-22==1.0.0b157 and constructs because these package versions have conflicting dependencies.

The conflict is caused by:
    The user requested constructs
    aws-cdk-lib 2.17.0 depends on constructs<11.0.0 and >=10.0.0
    cdk8s 2.2.48 depends on constructs<11.0.0 and >=10.0.0
    cdk8s-plus-22 1.0.0b157 depends on constructs<4.0.0 and >=3.3.249

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with cdk-for-kubernetes-cdk8s- installed or the issue environment reproducible.
